title: WIP - Setup Codecov coverage
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Setup code coverage testing via Codecov.  I'm having difficulties getting this to work for Buildah, but think it might here due to how the e2e testing is setup in libpod.   I've marked it a WIP for now, if it works, we can evaluate it's usefulness and timeliness.

comments.body: bot's ignoring me, closing and reopening to retest.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Passes tests, but not sure it generated anything useful.

It's getting closer.  It pushed something out to the codecov.io site (https://codecov.io/gh/projectatomic/libpod/commit/e2eac965590c23fab5a35763dabe3030169a816a) but the data that got there isn't making the report generation software happy for some reason.  I've repinged codecov support again, hoping it's just something easy that I missed.
Ugh, gotta stop hitting that close button.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 8493dba) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Can you come back to this PR and update it.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably c8c3977) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat, is anything blocking this PR? Having code coverage would be great.

@vrothberg This is a background project for me.  I keep pecking at it, my latest attempt was to create a small go project and just try to get the coverage to go there.  That worked.  Now I need to try and apply what I learned from there to Buildah and Podman.  Currently Podman runs the tests just fine, but the file that has the coverage data in it is always empty.  I think that's due to us running the tests in a container that's spun out of Travis rather than just running the tests directly in Travis.  I've asked for help from the codecov.io team, they reviewed our setup and didn't know what was happening.  I'm not sure how hard they dug though.  

So since Buildah doesn't do a similar setup of containers, I've been looking more at that as a start, then hope to get it back here in Podman.  However that to has an empty coverage report.  My thinking there was to next try just getting a small test to run and report in Buildah and then hopefully expand from there back in Podman. If you've any tips or thoughts, happy to hear them.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 4073541) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat what would you like to do with this one? Close?

@baude I'd like to still keep it around if for no other reason as a reminder to myself.  However given the removal of travis and the fact that codecov.io lives there, this will have to be completely revamped.  However, that might be a good thing.

@TomSweeneyRedHat: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

I'm going to nuke this PR.  codecov.io lives in Travis CI which we're no longer using.  I think we'll have to find another avenue.

I managed to get codecov.io working on my own private project, need to continue on in Buildah as it still has Travis.  Will circle back to podman/libpod later if I get time and someone doesn't beat me to it.
